'''Thomas Mikaele''' (born 11 January 1998) is a professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|pr}} for the [[North Queensland Cowboys]] in the [[National Rugby League]] (NRL).

He previously played for the [[Wests Tigers]], [[Gold Coast Titans]] and [[Warrington Wolves]].

==Background==
Mikaele was born in [[Auckland]], [[New Zealand]], and is of [[Samoans|Samoan]] descent.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/warrington-wolves-thomas-mikaele-samoa-world-cup|title=Warrington's proud Samoan Thomas Mikaele with a World Cup ambition|date=8 June 2022|website=Love Rugby League}}</ref>

He grew up in [[Ipswich, Queensland]] and played his junior rugby league for the [[Goodna Eagles]]. From 2015 to 2016, while attending [[Keebra Park State High School]], he was selected twice for the [[Australian Schoolboys rugby league team|Australian Schoolboys]].<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.qt.com.au/news/aussie-schoolboy-links-with-tigers/3083379/|title = Aussie schoolboy links with Tigers|newspaper = The Courier Mail|date = 29 August 2016|last1 = Benwilmott|first1 = By}}</ref>

==Playing career==
In 2014, Mikaele played for the [[Ipswich Jets]] in the [[Cyril Connell Cup]], before moving up to their [[Mal Meninga Cup]] team in 2015. In 2015, he was selected in the [[Queensland under-18 rugby league team|Queensland under-18]] team, scoring a try in their 22–18 win over New South Wales.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.qrl.com.au/news/2015/06/02/queensland-under-18-team/|title = Queensland Under 18 team|date = 2 June 2015}}</ref> In 2016, he again represented the Queensland under-18 team, starting at prop in a loss to New South Wales.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.qrl.com.au/news/2016/06/08/qld-under-18-team/|title=QLD Under 18 team|date=8 June 2016}}</ref>

In 2017, Mikaele joined the [[Wests Tigers]], playing for their [[NRL Under-20s]] and [[Jersey Flegg Cup]] sides.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.weststigers.com.au/news/2017/04/16/holden-cup-team-update-round-7/|title=Holden Cup Team Update: Round 7|date=16 April 2017}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nswrl.com.au/news/2019/03/12/debutant-watch--blayke-brailey-gets-the-nod/|title = DEBUTANT WATCH | Blayke Brailey gets the nod|date = 12 March 2019}}</ref>

===2019===
In Round 1 of the [[2019 NRL season]], Mikaele made his NRL debut against the [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles]] at [[Leichhardt Oval]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2019/03/12/round-1-nrl-team-lists/ |title=Round 1 NRL team lists|publisher=NRL.com|date=12 March 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/league/news/brendan-elliot-nrl-manly-sea-eagles-wests-tiger/ifnecw5psl9o1xxz2urgz7cme/|title=Brendan Elliot's minute of madness bombs a certain try|website=Sporting News|date=16 March 2019 }}</ref> He made 23 appearances over the course of the season, only missing one game in first grade, and re-signed with the Tigers until the end of the 2021 season.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.weststigers.com.au/news/2019/05/31/thomas-mikaele-re-signs-with-wests-tigers/|title = Thomas Mikaele re-signs with Wests Tigers|date = 31 May 2019}}</ref> Starting the season from the bench, he finished playing in the starting team at prop. He was one of the competition's leaders in attracting defenders, with 4 needed for 65% of his attacking runs.<ref name=kera>{{cite web| work= nrl.com | title='I couldn't see 15 metres in front of me': Mikaele's rookie hurdle|author=Alicia Newton | date=10 March 2020| url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2020/03/11/i-couldnt-see-15-metres-in-front-of-me-mikaeles-rookie-hurdle/}}</ref>

At season's end, Mikaele was diagnosed with [[keratoconus]]. He said, "I couldn't see about 15 metres in front of me so a ball coming to me was hard to catch at times or something happening further up the field. I didn't realise I had eye problems beyond just needing glasses. I started having a lot of handling errors from last year and then over the off-season. I noticed at training I couldn't see as far even with my contacts in."<ref name=kera />

===2020===
Mikaele played 18 games for Wests in the [[2020 NRL season]].  The club missed the finals by finishing 11th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/wests-tigers-season-review-peter-sterling-luke-brooks-moses-mbye-james-tamou-michael-maguire/0ee11d5b-9955-45a8-aa24-b30a25fd3d49/|title=Wests Tigers doomed to another finals miss in 2021, Peter Sterling predicts|website=wwos.nine.com.au|date=30 September 2020 }}</ref>

===2021===
Mikaele played a total of 19 games for the [[Wests Tigers]] in the [[2021 NRL season]] as the club finished a disappointing 13th and missed the finals.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.news.com.au/sport/nrl/wests-tigers-fans-savage-the-clubs-newlook-logo/news-story/83f356c5f7c0d336d950d750f4e054b5/|title=Wests Tigers fans savage the club's new-look logo|newspaper=News.com.au|date=6 October 2021|last1=Conrad|first1=Alex}}</ref>

===2022===
On 23 May, he was granted an immediate release from his Wests Tigers contract to join English side [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] on a two-and-a-half-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2022-dolphins-signings-edrick-lee-2023-wayne-bennett-squad-redclifee-queensland-maroons-top-30/news-story/7bf566b4338a5b790a443f694ff9eda3/|title=Broncos extend in-demand centre; Tiger makes sudden exit for Super League — Transfer Centre|website=www.foxsports.com.au|date=23 May 2022 }}</ref>

===2023===
On 31 March, Mikaele was granted an immediate release from his Warrington contract on compassionate grounds. Mikaele then signed a contract to join the [[Gold Coast Titans|Gold Coast]]. Mikaele left Warrington despite the club sitting on top of the Super League table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/wolves/wolvesnews/23695991.warrington-wolves-confirm-thomas-mikaele-short-term-return/|title=Warrington confirm Mikaele short-term return|website=warringtonwolves.com|date=2 August 2023 }}</ref> While with the Titans, Mikaele played just one game, spending the majority of his time with the [[Burleigh Bears]] in the [[Queensland Cup]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.titans.com.au/news/2023/06/05/affiliate-wrap-burleigh-tweed-go-down-in-tough-contests/|title=Affiliate wrap: Burleigh, Tweed go down in tough contests|date=5 June 2023|website=GC Titans}}</ref>

On 2 August, Warrington re-signed Mikaele for the remainder of the season, three days after coach [[Daryl Powell]] left the club by mutual consent following a run of eight losses in nine games.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/wolves/wolvesnews/23689336.warrington-wolves-confirm-departure-daryl-powell/|title=Wire confirm departure of Daryl Powell|website=warringtonguardian.co.uk|date=30 July 2023 }}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://warringtonwolves.com/news/2023/march/Mikaele-To-Join-Titans/|title=Mikaele to join the Gold Coast Titans|website=warringtonguardian.co.uk|date=31 March 2023 }}</ref>

On 7 December, he joined the [[North Queensland Cowboys]] on a one-year development contract.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.cowboys.com.au/news/2023/12/07/mikaele-joins-cowboys/|title=Mikaele joins Cowboys|date=7 December 2023|website=NQ Cowboys}}</ref>

===2024===
On 27 February, after two tries in a trial against [[Canberra Raiders|Canberra]], Mikaele was promoted to the Cowboys' Top 30 squad.<ref>{{Cite web | title=Archived copy | url=https://twitter.com/nthqldcowboys/status/1762373939253379323 | archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240227090150/https://twitter.com/nthqldcowboys/status/1762373939253379323 | archive-date=2024-02-27}}</ref>
Mikaele played 12 games for North Queensland in the [[2024 NRL season]] as they finished 5th on the table and qualified for the finals.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nine.com.au/sport/nrl/news-2024-the-mole-exclusive-north-queensland-cowboys-season-review-20240924-p5kcxq.html/|title=The Mole: Cowboys show improvement in 2024 but worrying finals stat sparks thorny question|website=www.nine.com.au|date=23 September 2024 }}</ref>

=== 2026 ===
On 16 March, the Cowboys announced that they had re-signed Mikaele until the end of 2028.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2026-03-16 |title=Cowboys re-sign Mikaele |url=https://www.cowboys.com.au/news/2026/03/16/cowboys-re-sign-mikaele/ |access-date=2026-03-17 |website=North Queensland Cowboys |language=en}}</ref>
